headline: Glass City Metropark urban campground — opening 2026
paragraphs: Metroparks Toledo is moving into the build phase on a new urban campground on the East Toledo riverfront, sited between the National Museum of the Great Lakes and the Craig Memorial Bridge, adjacent to the Glass City Marina that Metroparks already operates. The campground will be small but full-service — RV hookups, tent sites, cabins, and a pavilion. Funding stack: a Land and Water Conservation Fund federal grant pays for the access road and campsites; a ~$1M grant from the Lucas County Land Bank (out of $3.2M from the State of Ohio) covers site cleanup. Per Chief Outreach Officer Matt Killam, the campground is part of the broader Glass City Riverwalk project.
why_it_matters: An urban riverfront campground is an unusual asset for a Midwestern downtown — and boaters from the adjacent marina sharing the pavilion makes for a meaningfully different visitor pattern than Toledo currently captures. It also locks in another contiguous public-investment parcel along the East Toledo riverfront, the next neighborhood in line after Vistula for the Riverwalk-driven private-investment effect. Expect East Toledo land assemblies and small-business positioning to start showing up in the next 12 months.
search_terms: Glass City Metropark urban campground Toledo East Side Marina Land Bank
